title: [Workflows] Incorrect value being set on output during failed workflow
body:
When a Workflow throws an exception which puts the workflow into a `failed` state, the value of `dapr.workflow.failure.error_message` is copied into `dapr.workflow.output` which I believe is wrong behaviour.

IMO If a workflow has not completed successfully, the `output` should be undefined/null

## In what area(s)?

/area runtime

## What version of Dapr?

1.16

## Expected Behavior

`"dapr.workflow.output"` is set to empty value or something that represents null e.g.`""`

## Actual Behavior

```json
{
    "instanceID": "0-210",
    "workflowName": "FanOutWorkflow",
    "createdAt": "2025-09-26T21:48:05.974667500Z",
    "lastUpdatedAt": "2025-09-26T21:48:07.122787625Z",
    "runtimeStatus": "FAILED",
    "properties": {
        "dapr.workflow.failure.error_message": "Task 'SuperSlowActivity' (#0) failed with an unhandled exception: bomb",
        "dapr.workflow.failure.error_type": "Dapr.Workflow.WorkflowTaskFailedException",
        "dapr.workflow.input": "{\"RandomData\":\"b4cf9ba7-f925-4190-831f-fa1bdf77d40f\",\"Itterations\":200,\"Data\":null}",
        "dapr.workflow.output": "Task 'SuperSlowActivity' (#0) failed with an unhandled exception: bomb"
    }
}
```

cc @JoshVanL 

<!-- Briefly describe what is actually happening -->
